data.description:
## Why
rebar is event-per-file-per-commit: every mutation = one git commit (closer to
git-issue, the documented high-bloat counterexample). Re-enabling stock gc (epic
minor-haste-ogle) fixes *reclaim* of object bloat, but the growth *rate* is set at
write time. The durable way to bound growth is to create fewer objects at the
source — exactly what git-bug does.

## What
Batch multiple mutations into a single git blob/commit per logical operation, the
git-bug `operationPack` pattern (multiple ops serialized into one blob -> one
commit per Commit()), instead of one commit per event. Must preserve the
append-only / merge-as-union (CRDT) convergence the store relies on, and the
globally-unique event identity used by replay/dedup.

Prior art (verifiable):
- git-bug operationPack: https://github.com/git-bug/git-bug/blob/master/entity/dag/operation_pack.go
- git-bug entity commit/merge: https://github.com/git-bug/git-bug/blob/master/entity/dag/entity.go

## Scope
In: a batched-write primitive + adoption on the highest-churn write paths (import,
reconciler apply, multi-event transactions). Out: changing the per-ticket event
schema or the union/replay semantics.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A batch-commit primitive writes N events in one commit, preserving per-event identity, replay, and union-merge convergence
- [ ] Adopted on at least the bulk paths (import; reconciler batch apply)
- [ ] Object-count reduction demonstrated on a synthetic many-event run (before/after)
- [ ] No regression: replay, dedup, and cross-clone convergence tests green
data.title: Write-time op batching: fewer commits per mutation (git-bug operationPack pattern)
